ideas.title: Claude can make the image asset now
ideas.about: Turn the Higgsfield/Claude concept into a practical business-output demo, not just a tool announcement. Example: ask Claude to plan a local coffee shop promo, then connect Higgsfield to create the hero image or short visual.
ideas.evidence: Braydon's May 15 Higgsfield post is already the strongest new own-account signal from this scrape: 26.5K views, 1,287 saves, 297 shares. Justyn's Higgsfield MCP + Claude post is also a competitor wave at 51.6K views, 3,000 saves, 665 shares.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Claude can make the visual now
ideas.spokenHook: Claude used to help you think through the campaign. Now it can help create the image too, and this is where it gets useful for real businesses.
ideas.why: It combines Braydon's current winner with a recent competitor wave, and it keeps the angle grounded in a visible output instead of abstract AI news.

ideas.title: One-person company, but make it useful
ideas.about: Explain the one-person-company agent stack trend through a concrete operator lens: one person can research, draft, design, follow up, and measure with AI as leverage. Avoid promising replacement. Frame it as AI extending the business owner's expertise.
ideas.evidence: brandnat's recent GStack/Y-Combinator post is the biggest competitor signal in today's scan: 270.5K views, 18.5K likes, 984 comments, 4,199 shares, 21,784 saves. Caption: open-source project for one-person companies that feels like hiring 23 roles.
ideas.spokenHook: This does not mean one person replaces a whole team. It means one smart operator can finally get help with the parts that used to slow them down.
ideas.why: The topic has huge current competitor proof, but Braydon can make it warmer and clearer by translating the hype into a practical mentor-style framework.

ideas.title: The context file your AI keeps missing
ideas.about: Show a simple `CLAUDE.md` or context handoff file that tells an AI who the audience is, what the brand voice is, what not to say, and what the desired output looks like.
ideas.evidence: Taki's Claude memory/handoff post from May 14 hit 21.9K views, 766 saves, and 288 comments. Braydon's own Claude education cluster is still strong: Claude Dreams at 38.2K, Claude free courses at 18.4K, and Claude + Codex together at 20.9K.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Your AI needs a context file
ideas.spokenHook: If your AI keeps giving you generic answers, the problem might not be the tool. It might be the context you forgot to give it.
ideas.why: Saves and comments are high on context-memory content, and Braydon can make the workflow beginner-friendly without dumbing it down.

ideas.title: Turn prompts into a business asset library
ideas.about: Instead of dumping 5,000 image prompts, show how to create a small reusable prompt library for one business: product photo, event flyer, testimonial graphic, email header, and social post.
ideas.evidence: Justyn's ChatGPT image-prompt post is a current monitoring signal at 8.4K views with 253 saves and 207 comments. Braydon's own ChatGPT flyers post reached 11.5K views and 307 saves, and his real-estate image post reached 7.7K views and 197 saves.
ideas.spokenHook: A giant prompt list is cool, but a small prompt library for your actual business is way more useful.
ideas.why: Prompt-library content is getting comments, but Braydon's stronger angle is turning it into a usable business system.

competitors.text: 80% of companies laying off employees because of AI saw zero financial benefit. Gartner's data backs this up. Most of those "AI layoff" announcements weren't about AI at all. They over-hired when money was cheap and interest rates were low. Blaming AI sounds smarter than admitting you missed revenue targets. The companies winning with AI aren't using it to replace people. They're using it as a redistribution layer. Support teams handle fewer tickets, but each interaction carries more weight because the easy ones are already automated. It's the right mindset. It's what I teach. It's how I run my own startup. Are AI layoffs real or PR spin? How do companies win with AI? What's the difference between AI replacement and AI redistribution? #ai #business #startup #entrepreneur #technology

Based on everything you know about me, tell me who you think I'm pretending to be versus my true self. 2. Be brutally honest and tell me my deepest insecurities. 3. What is the 1 cycle I'm stuck in keeping me average forever? 4. If I fix this, what does the most successful version of my life look like in 5 years? ChatGPT paints 2 wildly different pictures of what your life looks like. What did ChatGPT say about you? Have you ever asked AI to be brutally honest with you? What's the 1 thing holding you back from your best life? #chatgpt #ai #chatgptprompts #psychology #aitools

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: 4 AI skills separating winners from losers in the AI gold rush. It's not about which tools you use. It's not about keeping up with AI news. It's 4 mental skills most people skip. 1. Change your default reaction. When you're stuck or confused, ask AI first. Before Google. Before asking someone else. 2. Treat AI like a new hire. Don't expect it to know everything on day 1. Give it feedback over time. 3. Continuously improve your AI setup. Ask AI to reflect on your conversation and update its own skills. Your CLAUDE.md should get better every single day. 4. Stop trying to replace entire roles. Use AI to get informed. So when you sit down with your accountant, you're asking better questions. How to use AI productively? AI skills for beginners? How to stop chasing AI tools? #ai #aitools #productivity #entrepreneur #startup

Here's how to fix it with a custom handoff skill that actually works and allows you to transfer context from one chat to another. #ai #prompting #claude #productivity #artificialintelligence Keywords: Claude memory, AI handoff, custom skills, conversation continuity, prompt engineering, AI workflows, context retention, Claude tips, AI productivity, automation, machine learning, AI tools
competitors.topic: The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill
competitors.onScreenTitle: The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill
competitors.spokenHook: Claude forgets everything after the first 20 messages. Here's the fastest fix so in thropic the creators of Claude advise that after 20 or 30 messages with Claude you start a brand new conversation but the problem is when you transfer to a new conversation sometimes Claude leaves out important details and so I I created a new skill called the handoff skill that makes sure that Claude captures all the important information and seamlessly transfers it to any new conversation and this handoff skill generates the summary using the exact format below and it's able to capture the core contexts key decisions what's pending and the tone and style of that conversation and then what it does is that it creates this beautiful hand off summary that it can feed directly inside of the next Claude conversation so now whenever you have a really long conversation inside of Claude all you need to do is type in this skill forward slash hand off and it will generate this perfect hand off summary that you can then copy and put into a brand new conversation with Claude to ensure that you start exactly where you left off without losing any context at all so if you want this exact handoff skill and a full guide on the best Claude skills and how to optimize your Claude setup just comment the word Claude and I'll go ahead and send it over to you and as always if you like this sort Of content and you want to stay ahead in the world of AI, make sure that you give me a follow.

competitors.creator: taki.gpt
competitors.text: Comment “CLAUDE” to get the best Claude optimization and setup guide on the internet. Boris Cherny created Claude Code and he just shared 10 tips on how he uses it. Here are the key takeaways. First, always start with Plan Mode. Do this before any code. Just press Shift+Tab to turn it on. Iterate on your plan. Make it perfect. This stops many errors later. Think of it like drawing a map first. Second, run multiple Claude Code sessions. Use each for a different project part. Then merge them at the end. Let AI do the merge. You finish projects three to five times faster. It’s like having extra hands working at once. Third, the agent makes a mistake? Tell it to update its own claude.md file. Do this every time. It learns. The same mistake won’t happen again. Simple as fixing a recipe note. Fourth, build sub-agents. Use them to automate your work. One agent writes code. Another reviews it. A third deploys it. They all run on their own. Picture a team passing a baton smoothly. The good news? These tips fit any workflow. Because anytime Claude helps with one part, it speeds up the rest: #borischerny #claudeai #claudecode #claudetips #aitipsandtricks
competitors.topic: 10 Pro Tips for Claude Code / From the creator of Claude Code!
competitors.onScreenTitle: 10 Pro Tips for Claude Code / From the creator of Claude Code!
competitors.spokenHook: The creator of Claude Code Boris Cherny just dropped 10 tips on how he uses Claude Code and here are the key takeaways I've personally tried all of them and I'll show you the top five of his tips if you want to get the most out of Claude and stay till the end because the last one is definitely the most impactful and if you like this sort of content and you want to get the most out of your a I experience make sure that you give me a follow tip number one is that he runs multiple Claude agents in parallel inside of his terminal and inside of the Claude desktop app it self and running multiple agents is useful because you can have each agent take care of specific responsibilities like one for coding one for testing one for design one for iteration and one for review tip No. 2 is he uses a shared Claude MD file which is basically a context file that teaches Claude everything about who he is, how he gets work done, his business etc and when you share that with your entire team everybody then has access to that same context so the results that they're gonna create are gonna be similar tip No.

competitors.creator: taki.gpt
competitors.text: Your AI is only as smart as the context you give it. A solid Context or Claude.md file will make your AI 10x better with personalized outputs. Comment “Claude” and I’ll send you the best Claude guide on the internet. #ai #claude #chatGPT #AIforbusines
competitors.topic: This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately

briefMarkdown:
# TikTok Ideas, May 16, 2026

Generated: May 16, 2026 at 6:42 AM MT

Source of truth: Hermes-native workspace at `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/`.

## Best ideas for today

**Claude can make the image asset now** `🌊🔁 V2/reframe`
- **What it's about:** Turn the Higgsfield/Claude concept into a practical business-output demo, not just a tool announcement. Example: ask Claude to plan a local coffee shop promo, then connect Higgsfield to create the hero image or short visual.
- **Evidence:** Braydon's May 15 Higgsfield post is already the strongest new own-account signal from this scrape: 26.5K views, 1,287 saves, 297 shares. Justyn's Higgsfield MCP + Claude post is also a competitor wave at 51.6K views, 3,000 saves, 665 shares.
- **On-screen title:** Claude can make the visual now
- **Spoken hook:** Claude used to help you think through the campaign. Now it can help create the image too, and this is where it gets useful for real businesses.
- **Content type:** tool breakdown / tutorial
- **Why this should work:** It combines Braydon's current winner with a recent competitor wave, and it keeps the angle grounded in a visible output instead of abstract AI news.

**One-person company, but make it useful** `🌊 news-adjacent`
- **What it's about:** Explain the one-person-company agent stack trend through a concrete operator lens: one person can research, draft, design, follow up, and measure with AI as leverage. Avoid promising replacement. Frame it as AI extending the business owner's expertise.
- **Evidence:** brandnat's recent GStack/Y-Combinator post is the biggest competitor signal in today's scan: 270.5K views, 18.5K likes, 984 comments, 4,199 shares, 21,784 saves. Caption: open-source project for one-person companies that feels like hiring 23 roles.
- **On-screen title:** One person, 23 AI helpers
- **Spoken hook:** This does not mean one person replaces a whole team. It means one smart operator can finally get help with the parts that used to slow them down.
- **Content type:** hot take / tool breakdown
- **Why this should work:** The topic has huge current competitor proof, but Braydon can make it warmer and clearer by translating the hype into a practical mentor-style framework.

**The context file your AI keeps missing** `🌊🔁 V2/reframe`
- **What it's about:** Show a simple `CLAUDE.md` or context handoff file that tells an AI who the audience is, what the brand voice is, what not to say, and what the desired output looks like.
- **Evidence:** Taki's Claude memory/handoff post from May 14 hit 21.9K views, 766 saves, and 288 comments. Braydon's own Claude education cluster is still strong: Claude Dreams at 38.2K, Claude free courses at 18.4K, and Claude + Codex together at 20.9K.
- **On-screen title:** Your AI needs a context file
- **Spoken hook:** If your AI keeps giving you generic answers, the problem might not be the tool. It might be the context you forgot to give it.
- **Content type:** quick tip / tutorial
- **Why this should work:** Saves and comments are high on context-memory content, and Braydon can make the workflow beginner-friendly without dumbing it down.

**Turn prompts into a business asset library** `🌊🔁 V2/reframe`
- **What it's about:** Instead of dumping 5,000 image prompts, show how to create a small reusable prompt library for one business: product photo, event flyer, testimonial graphic, email header, and social post.
- **Evidence:** Justyn's ChatGPT image-prompt post is a current monitoring signal at 8.4K views with 253 saves and 207 comments. Braydon's own ChatGPT flyers post reached 11.5K views and 307 saves, and his real-estate image post reached 7.7K views and 197 saves.
- **On-screen title:** Stop saving random prompts
- **Spoken hook:** A giant prompt list is cool, but a small prompt library for your actual business is way more useful.
- **Content type:** tutorial / myth-bust
- **Why this should work:** Prompt-library content is getting comments, but Braydon's stronger angle is turning it into a usable business system.

## Quick posting recommendation

1. Post the Claude visual asset workflow first. It is the clearest current winner because Braydon's own Higgsfield post is already moving.
2. Use the one-person-company agent stack as the second slot if you want a bigger trend piece.
3. Keep the context-file idea as a reliable educational backup.

## Research notes

- **Braydon performance:** Latest scrape returned 30 recent posts. Top recent performers are Claude UGC at 163.3K, Claude + Blotato at 60.9K, Claude Dreams at 38.2K, today's Claude/Higgsfield visual post at 26.5K, and Claude + Codex together at 20.9K.
- **Competitor wave:** The biggest clean signal is brandnat's one-person-company/GStack post at 270.5K views. Other current useful signals: Justyn's Higgsfield/Claude post at 51.6K, Taki's Claude memory handoff post at 21.9K, Taki's Claude Code tips post at 10.3K, and Digital Samaritan's Google AI post at 15.1K, though the Google item was marked as an ad by the scraper and should be treated as pattern context only.
- **News/early catch:** Fast X scan ran, but no stronger official AI launch displaced the TikTok performance signals. Today's recommendations are mainly wave-ride plus V2/reframe, not pure news.

## Extraction and coverage check

- **Braydon scrape metrics:** Success, 30 latest non-pinned posts returned.
- **Competitor scrape metrics:** Success, 50 posts returned across all 10 configured handles: sabrina_ramonov, rileybrown.ai, tony.aube, the.rachelwoods, nocode.joshua, marcinteodoru, taki.gpt, digitalsamaritan, brandnat, justyn.ai.
- **On-screen titles:** Existing cache plus this run covered all idea-evidence posts. New cover OCR was completed for Braydon's Higgsfield post and three uncached competitor covers. Some lower-priority scan items remain caption/performance-only in the report.
- **Spoken hooks:** WebVTT/subtitle extraction was available for most cached/evidence posts. Braydon's newest Higgsfield scrape had missing subtitle links in the asset helper, so its spoken-hook layer is unavailable in this run and should be treated as caption/performance evidence plus cover-title evidence.
- **Paid transcription:** Disabled. No paid TikTok transcription add-on was used.

## Saved artifacts

- Daily idea file: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/ideas/2026-05-16-tiktok-ideas.md`
- Hook cache updated: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/data/tiktok-hooks-cache.json`
- Asset manifests: `/tmp/braydon_extract_manifest.json`, `/tmp/competitor_extract_manifest.json`
